factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin products, particularly in the Rest of World segment, where sales in China and India are growing due to increased demand for water treatment and high-end products.

evidence.text: Sales in China were also positively impacted by lower channel inventory reductions in 2021 compared to 2020. Products with higher selling prices, including super-quiet gas tankless water heaters and water treatment products that deliver filtered water at a faster flow rate, contributed to sales gains. Sales in India increased approximately 31% compared to 2020, which was significantly impacted by the pandemic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin products, particularly in the Rest of World segment, with growing sales in China and India.

factors.summary: The fastest-growing end markets are in China and India, driven by demand for water treatment products and high-end appliances. The North America market is showing a slowdown in residential water heater demand.

evidence.text: In our Rest of World segment, after strong growth in 2021, we expect 2022 sales in China to increase approximately five percent in local currency compared with 2021 driven by demand for our residential and commercial water treatment products, including our replacement filters, as well as rangehoods and cooktops. We assume China currency rates will stay at levels similar to 2021. In our North America segment, after approximately eight percent growth each year in 2021 and 2020, we expect residential industry water heater volumes will be down approximately two percent in 2022 compared with 2021 as we believe that industry demand will normalize to more historical growth rates.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Fastest-growing end markets in China and India, driven by demand for water treatment products and high-end appliances, despite a slowdown in North America.

factors.summary: Productivity initiatives, such as operational efficiency improvements, are being pursued to manage supply chain disruptions and cost pressures, but challenges remain due to ongoing supply chain issues.

evidence.text: Our global supply chain management team continued to navigate through supply chain and logistics challenges in 2021. We have seen supply constraints for certain components and raw materials used in our operations, as well as limited container and trucking capacity, and port congestion and delays. While supply chain issues moderated as we moved into 2022, we remain in close contact with our suppliers and logistics providers to troubleshoot, manage and resolve bottlenecks, as the environment remains unpredictable, particularly with the surge in the Omicron variant of COVID-19.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Productivity initiatives are being pursued, but ongoing supply chain issues pose challenges.

factors.summary: Management appears cautiously optimistic, with share repurchases and dividend increases indicating confidence, but also acknowledges risks such as supply chain disruptions and geopolitical factors.

evidence.text: We expect free cash flow to be between $500 million to $525 million in 2022. Free cash flow is a non U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) measure and is described in more detail in the Non-GAAP Measures section below. We continue to monitor developments on an ongoing basis and have taken proactive measures to focus on cash, manage working capital, and reduce costs. On January 25, 2022, the Board of Directors approved adding 3,500,000 shares of common stock to the existing discretionary share repurchase authority. Including the additional shares, we have approximately 6.8 million shares available for repurchase as of the date of the Board of Directors' approval. We intend to repurchase approximately $400 million of our common stock in 2022 through a combination of 10b5-1 plans and open market purchases. We paid dividends of $1.06 per share in 2021 compared with $0.98 per share in 2020. We increased our dividend by eight percent in the fourth quarter of 2021, and the five-year compound annual growth rate of our dividend payment is approximately 17 percent.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is cautiously optimistic with share repurchases and dividend increases, despite acknowledging risks.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential supply chain disruptions, particularly from the surge in the Omicron variant, and geopolitical factors such as trade tensions and conflicts.

evidence.text: While supply chain issues moderated as we moved into 2022, we remain in close contact with our suppliers and logistics providers to troubleshoot, manage and resolve bottlenecks, as the environment remains unpredictable, particularly with the surge in the Omicron variant of COVID-19. We continue to monitor developments on an ongoing basis and have taken proactive measures to focus on cash, manage working capital, and reduce costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks involve supply chain disruptions and geopolitical tensions, indicating potential challenges.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is clear, focusing on core businesses, acquisitions in high-growth regions, and product innovation to drive future growth.

evidence.text: We seek to continue to grow our core residential and commercial water heating, boiler and water treatment businesses throughout the world. This includes focusing on acquisitions that are related to our core business. Consistent with this strategy, we acquired Giant Factories, Inc. (Giant), a Canada-based manufacturer of residential and commercial water heaters, on October 19, 2021, for $199 million, subject to customary adjustments, using a combination of debt and cash. The acquisition fits squarely in our core capabilities, supplements our presence in Canada and enhances our capacity and distribution in the region. We will also continue to look for opportunities to add to our existing operations in high growth regions demonstrated by our previous introductions of water treatment products in India and range hoods and cooktops in China.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategy with focus on core businesses, acquisitions, and innovation suggests growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, foreign exchange rates, supply chain disruptions, and economic conditions, particularly in China and India.

evidence.text: Our sales in China were favorably impacted by approximately $58 million in 2021 compared to 2020, due to the appreciation of the Chinese currency compared to the U.S. dollar. We assume China currency rates will stay at levels similar to 2021. The company is exposed to foreign exchange movements which could impact margins and translation of results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Exposure to commodity prices, exchange rates, and supply chain issues poses risks, particularly in key markets.

factors.summary: A specific near-term catalyst is the expected contribution from the Giant acquisition, which is anticipated to add $0.06-$0.08 to EPS in 2022.

evidence.text: The acquisition fits squarely in our core capabilities, supplements our presence in Canada and enhances our capacity and distribution in the region. Giant contributed $22.9 million of sales and approximately $0.01 in earnings per share (EPS) to our results in 2021 and we expect Giant will contribute approximately $0.06-$0.08 to our EPS in 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expected contribution from Giant acquisition adds value to EPS, indicating a positive catalyst.

factors.summary: CapEx is expected to increase slightly in 2022 compared to 2021, signaling confidence in future growth and operational efficiency improvements.

evidence.text: We project our 2022 capital expenditures will be between $75 and $80 million and expect depreciation and amortization will be approximately $80 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased CapEx signals investment in future growth and efficiency improvements.

factors.summary: Debt leverage has increased due to the Giant acquisition and share repurchases, with the leverage ratio rising from 5.8% to 9.7%.

evidence.text: Our total debt increased by $83.5 million from $113.2 million at December 31, 2020 to $196.7 million at December 31, 2021. The increase in debt balances was due to our acquisition of Giant and repurchases of our common stock. Our leverage, as measured by the ratio of total debt to total capitalization, calculated excluding operating lease liabilities, was 9.7 percent at December 31, 2021, compared with 5.8 percent at December 31, 2020.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased debt leverage due to acquisition and repurchases could pose financial risks.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are being prioritized with increased share repurchases and dividend payments, signaling management confidence in the company's future performance.

evidence.text: We expect to repurchase approximately $400 million of our common stock in 2022 through a combination of 10b5-1 plans and open market purchases. We paid dividends of $1.06 per share in 2021 compared with $0.98 per share in 2020. We increased our dividend by eight percent in the fourth quarter of 2021, and the five-year compound annual growth rate of our dividend payment is approximately 17 percent.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased share repurchases and dividends indicate confidence in the company's future performance.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes pose risks as new laws could increase costs and reduce demand. While the company is managing these changes, there is no specific information on the impact on operations or costs.

evidence.text: Federal, foreign, state, and local governments may adopt laws requiring a transition to non-fossil fuel sources, which could significantly increase manufacturing costs, impact demand, or put the company at a competitive disadvantage.
Our operations are governed by a number of federal, foreign, state, local and environmental laws [...] We have expended financial and managerial resources complying with such laws.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Regulatory changes could increase costs and reduce demand, posing significant risks.

factors.summary: The company faces compliance challenges with environmental regulations, which could impose operational constraints. While they have established reserves and insurance to manage current obligations, there is a risk of future costs.

evidence.text: Our products are subject to various environmental regulations and industry standards related to energy efficiency, emissions, labeling, and safety.

Expenditures related to environmental matters were not material in 2021 and we do not expect them to be material in any single year. We have reserves associated with environmental obligations at various facilities and we believe these reserves together with available insurance coverage are sufficient to cover reasonably anticipated remediation costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces compliance challenges with environmental regulations, which could impose operational constraints and pose future cost risks.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power, particularly in North America, with cumulative price increases of about 50% in 2021. Despite this success, there is evidence of a lag in fully recovering costs from customers.

evidence.text: As a response to cost inflation in materials and components, we announced price increases on water heaters in North America in 2021 that compound to approximately 50 percent.
Our higher expected sales are driven by pricing actions in North America and increased boiler and water treatment volumes within that region.
We expect sales in 2022 will benefit from our 2021 price increases, which had a cumulative effect on our water heater prices of approximately 50 percent.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power with significant price increases, contributing to revenue growth despite lagging cost recovery.

factors.summary: Input costs have increased significantly, particularly for materials like steel, which saw a 100% price increase in 2021. This rise has impacted the company's gross margins and product warranty reserve, outpacing their pricing actions and leading to margin compression.

evidence.text: The market prices for certain materials and components we purchase, primarily steel, have been volatile. U.S. steel index prices alone increased over 100 percent in 2021.
The increase in our reserve for product warranties in 2021 compared to the prior year was primarily due to increased steel prices and the acquisition of Giant.
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Critical Accounting Policies

evidence.text: The lower gross margin in 2021 was primarily due to higher steel and other material costs which outpaced our pricing actions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs have risen significantly, impacting gross margins and product warranty reserves, leading to margin compression.

factors.summary: The company faces significant geopolitical risks, particularly from trade tensions and political instability in key markets such as China and India. These risks include potential impacts from international tariffs, trade disputes, and geopolitical differences, which could negatively affect the company's financial condition and operations. Additionally, geopolitical risks could strain the supply chain and require ongoing monitoring and proactive measures to mitigate their effects.

evidence.text: Geopolitical risks include trade tensions and political instability in key markets like China and India.

International operations generally are subject to various risks, including: political, religious, and economic instability; [...] and differences in business practices. Unfavorable changes in the political, regulatory, or trade climate [...] could have a material adverse effect on our financial condition.
Negative impacts to our businesses from international tariffs, trade disputes and geopolitical differences; potential weakening in the high-efficiency boiler segment in the U.S.
We continue to monitor developments on an ongoing basis and have taken proactive measures to focus on cash, manage working capital, and reduce costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant geopolitical risks, including trade tensions and political instability, pose threats to financial condition and supply chain.

factors.summary: The company faces mixed economic conditions, with strong demand in regions like China and India, but overall economic uncertainty due to the pandemic, inflation, supply chain disruptions, potential recessions, and geopolitical factors. Economic conditions are a headwind, particularly from the impact of the Omicron variant of COVID-19, inflation, supply chain disruptions, and potential recessions.

evidence.text: A decline in economic activity, such as recession or economic downturn, in the U.S. and other regions in the world in which we do business, could adversely affect consumer confidence and spending patterns which could result in decreased demand for the products we sell.

Our 2022 guidance excludes the potential impacts from future acquisitions and assumes the recent surge of the Omicron variant subsides during the first quarter of 2022 and does not have a significant impact on our productivity or significantly impact the end markets that we serve.

In our Rest of World segment, after strong growth in 2021, we expect 2022 sales in China to increase approximately five percent in local currency compared with 2021 driven by demand for our residential and commercial water treatment products, including our replacement filters, as well as rangehoods and cooktops. We assume China currency rates will stay at levels similar to 2021. The company is exposed to foreign exchange movements which could impact margins and translation of results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Mixed economic conditions with uncertainties from the pandemic, inflation, supply chain disruptions, and potential recessions create headwinds.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed across different regions and product segments. While there is growth in North America's boiler and water treatment segments, as well as in China and India, there are potential declines in the North America residential water heater market and overall residential new construction activity due to economic conditions.

evidence.text: Residential new construction activity in North America and industry-wide replacement-related volume of water heaters have shown growth which could decline in the future.
Our higher expected sales are driven by pricing actions in North America and increased boiler and water treatment volumes within that region.

Sales in China increased by 32 percent in U.S. dollar terms and 24 percent in local currency in 2021 compared to 2020. In addition, our sales in China were favorably impacted by approximately $58 million in 2021 compared to 2020, due to the appreciation of the Chinese currency compared to the U.S. dollar. The increase in 2021 sales was primarily due to growth in our major product categories in China, including electric and gas tankless water heaters, and residential and commercial water treatment products, including replacement filters. Sales in India increased approximately 31% compared to 2020, which was significantly impacted by the pandemic.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Volume trends show growth in some regions but potential declines in others, indicating mixed signals.

factors.summary: The company is facing significant challenges in its supply chain, including disruptions due to transportation issues, raw material cost increases, and geopolitical factors. While there were some moderations in 2022, the situation remains unpredictable and vulnerable to external factors like the Omicron variant.

evidence.text: Disruptions to the commercial transportation network, including limited container and trucking capacity and port congestion, have increased supplier delivery times for materials and components to our facilities and, in some cases, our ability to timely ship to customers.

Our operations are governed by a number of federal, foreign, state, local and environmental laws [...] Although we believe that our operations are substantially in compliance with such laws and maintain procedures designed to maintain compliance, there are no assurances that substantial additional costs for compliance will not be incurred in the future.

Our global supply chain management team continued to navigate through supply chain and logistics challenges in 2021. We have seen supply constraints for certain components and raw materials used in our operations, as well as limited container and trucking capacity, and port congestion and delays. While supply chain issues moderated as we moved into 2022, we remain in close contact with our suppliers and logistics providers to troubleshoot, manage and resolve bottlenecks, as the environment remains unpredictable, particularly with the surge in the Omicron variant of COVID-19.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Challenges in supply chain due to disruptions and raw material cost increases, with ongoing unpredictability.

factors.summary: Demand trends are facing challenges, with a deceleration in the North America segment, particularly in the residential water heater market, expected to decline by 2% in 2022. Additionally, broader economic downturns and shifts in consumer behavior pose uncertainties, especially in commercial sectors.

evidence.text: Significant reductions in demand, particularly for our commercial products, or significant volatility in demand and a global economic recession that could reduce demand for our products.

In our North America segment, after approximately eight percent growth each year in 2021 and 2020, we expect residential industry water heater volumes will be down approximately two percent in 2022 compared with 2021 as we believe that industry demand will normalize to more historical growth rates.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends show deceleration in key segments and broader economic uncertainties, posing risks.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is showing mixed trends, with acceleration in overall revenue driven by pricing actions and increased volumes in North America, yet deceleration is observed in the North America segment due to expected declines in residential water heater volumes and flat to slightly down commercial water heater volumes in 2022.

evidence.text: As we begin 2022, we expect our consolidated sales to increase between 16 to 18 percent compared to 2021. Our higher expected sales are driven by pricing actions in North America and increased boiler and water treatment volumes within that region.

In our North America segment, after approximately eight percent growth each year in 2021 and 2020, we expect residential industry water heater volumes will be down approximately two percent in 2022 compared with 2021 as we believe that industry demand will normalize to more historical growth rates. We believe that commercial water heater industry volumes will be flat to slightly down in 2022 compared to 2021 as new construction and replacement installations level off.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth shows acceleration in some areas but deceleration in others, indicating mixed trends.

factors.summary: The company has managed labor costs through restructuring and cost-saving measures, including severance and restructuring expenses, but faces potential wage inflation due to a competitive labor market. Additionally, pension expenses, influenced by labor costs, have been managed through actuarial assumptions and plan changes, with adjustments to expected returns on plan assets and discount rates.

evidence.text: To align our business to market conditions in 2020, we recognized $7.7 million of pre-tax severance and restructuring expenses. The charges were comprised of $6.8 million severance costs and $0.9 million of other restructuring expenses. These activities are reflected in 'severance and restructuring expenses' in the accompanying financial statements.
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Critical Accounting Policies

evidence.text: In developing our expected return on plan assets, we evaluate our pension plan’s current and target asset allocation, the expected long-term rates of return of equity and bond indices and the actual historical returns of our pension plan. Our assumption for the expected return on plan assets was 6.25 and 6.75 percent in 2021 and 2020, respectively. The discount rate used to determine net periodic pension costs decreased to 2.47 percent in 2021 from 3.18 percent in 2020.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Costs are managed through restructuring but face risks from wage inflation and pension expenses.

factors.summary: The company has provided forward guidance indicating a positive outlook with expected consolidated sales growth of 16% to 18% in 2022, driven by pricing actions and increased volumes in North America. The guidance assumes the Omicron variant's impact will subside and excludes potential effects from future acquisitions.

evidence.text: As we begin 2022, we expect our consolidated sales to increase between 16 to 18 percent compared to 2021. Our higher expected sales are driven by pricing actions in North America and increased boiler and water treatment volumes within that region. We expect to achieve full-year earnings of between $1.56 and $1.76 per share and an adjusted EPS in the range of $3.35 and $3.55 per share. Our 2022 guidance excludes the potential impacts from future acquisitions and assumes the recent surge of the Omicron variant subsides during the first quarter of 2022 and does not have a significant impact on our productivity or significantly impact the end markets that we serve.

Combining all of these factors, we expect our consolidated sales to increase between 16 and 18 percent in 2022. Our guidance excludes the potential impacts from future acquisitions and assumes the recent surge of the Omicron variant subsides during the first quarter of 2022 and does not have a significant impact on our productivity or significantly impact the end markets that we serve.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Forward guidance indicates strong sales growth and excludes potential risks, suggesting a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are under pressure due to multiple factors including increased warranty reserves, rising material costs, supply chain issues, and potential pension expenses. These pressures are expected to continue into 2022, with no immediate signs of relief.

evidence.text: The increase in our reserve for product warranties in 2021 compared to the prior year was primarily due to increased steel prices and the acquisition of Giant.
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Critical Accounting Policies

evidence.text: Lowering the expected return on plan assets by 25 basis points would decrease our net pension income for 2021 by approximately $1.8 million. Lowering the discount rate by 25 basis points would increase our 2021 net pension income by approximately $0.7 million.

Our gross profit margin in 2021 of 37.0 percent declined compared to 38.3 percent in 2020. The lower gross margin in 2021 was primarily due to higher steel and other material costs which outpaced our pricing actions.
Segment margins were lower in 2021 primarily due to the rise in costs outpacing pricing actions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are under pressure from multiple factors, including rising costs and supply chain issues.

factors.summary: The company has implemented cost-saving measures, including pension plan termination and restructuring, which have contributed to margin improvements. However, these actions face challenges from rising costs in 2022.

evidence.text: In 2021, our Board of Directors approved the termination of our defined benefit pension plan (the Plan) with a termination date of December 31, 2021. [...] The Plan settlement, which we expect to complete in the fourth quarter of 2022, will accelerate the recognition of approximately $445 million, or $1.73 of EPS, of non-cash, pre-tax pension expenses. [...] We will continue to evaluate our actuarial assumptions at least annually, and we will adjust the assumptions as necessary.
evidence.subsection: Recent Accounting Pronouncements / Critical Accounting Policies

evidence.text: The increase in SG&A expenses in 2021 was primarily due to higher advertising, engineering and selling expenses and higher management incentive expenses related to higher earnings compared to 2020. Higher SG&A expenses in 2021 were partially offset by lower spending in China associated with headcount reductions, store closures and other cost-saving measures implemented during 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost-saving measures have contributed to margin improvements despite ongoing challenges.

factors.summary: The company is demonstrating strong free cash flow generation, with a notable increase from 2020 to 2021 and expectations for continued growth in 2022.

evidence.text: The following is a reconciliation of reported cash flow from operating activities to free cash flow (non-GAAP): [...] Twelve Months Ended, December 31, 2021 2020 Cash provided by operating activities (GAAP) $ 641.1 $ 562.1 Less: Capital expenditures (75.1) (56.8) Free cash flow (non-GAAP) $ 566.0 $ 505.3

We expect free cash flow to be between $500 million to $525 million in 2022. Free cash flow is a non U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) measure and is described in more detail in the Non-GAAP Measures section below.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong free cash flow generation with expectations for continued growth.
